questions.text: When you want to learn something new — whether it's a framework, a skill, or a random topic — how do you actually go about it? Do you dive into documentation, watch video courses, read books, build a side project, or prefer learning from people who know more than you?

When it comes to money beyond investing — things like spending guilt, saving habits, whether you track every dollar or just have a general sense — how do you think about your relationship with money? Are you someone who enjoys spending on experiences, pretty frugal overall, or somewhere in between?

If you could instantly become great at any creative skill — photography, music production, woodworking, painting, writing — what would you pick and why? Is there something that's been nagging at you to try, or that you've always admired in others?

Do you consider yourself someone who documents moments — taking photos, keeping a journal, or recording voice notes — or are you more of a 'live in the moment' person who doesn't think much about capturing experiences?

When you think about the 'dating' or romantic part of your life — whether you're currently dating or not — what matters most to you? Is it shared values, physical attraction, intellectual connection, someone who balances your personality, or something else entirely?
